summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity at install via postinstall script
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` and reads installer-owned identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to extract the developer's git user.email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It bundles these with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), os.platform(), and CI-provider markers into a JSON body and POSTs it via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — an anonymous Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's purported homepage (openai-agents-js.guide). A comment in the script claims the telemetry is 'fully anonymous — no credentials transmitted,' which directly contradicts the payload shape. The package name and author identity ('openai-agents-helpers', 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>') impersonate the official @openai/agents SDK ecosystem; OpenAI's real domain is openai.com, not openai-agents-js.guide. The combination of brand impersonation, install-time auto-execution, identity-file harvesting, and exfiltration to an anonymous Cloud Run URL fits a credential-harvest / targeted-follow-on-attack profile.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
